tools.description: Retrieves the available API groups and resources from a Kubernetes cluster. This is similar to running `kubectl api-resources`.

inputSchema.description: Request for retrieving Kubernetes API resources.

parent.description: Required. The cluster, which owns this collection of resource types. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/clusters/{cluster}

tools.name: list_k8s_api_resources

APIGroupDiscovery.description: APIGroupDiscovery is a discovery document for an API group.

name.description: The name of the resource type. e.g. "pods", "deployments", "services".

preferredVersion.description: The preferred version for this API group, in the form {group}/{version}.

versions.description: The list of versions for this API group, in the form {group}/{version}.

Status.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

outputSchema.description: APIGroupDiscoveryList is a list of API group discovery.

groups.description: The list of API group discovery.

tools.description: Checks whether an action is allowed on a Kubernetes resource. This is similar to running `kubectl auth can-i`.

inputSchema.description: Request for checking authorization for a Kubernetes resource.

namespace.description: Optional. The namespace of the resource. If not specified, "default" is used for namespace-scoped resources.

parent.description: Required. The cluster to check authorization against. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/clusters/{cluster}

resource.description: Optional. The name of the resource to check.

resourceType.description: Required. The type of resource to check. e.g. "pods", "deployments", "services".

verb.description: Required. The verb to check. e.g. "get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch", "delete".

tools.name: check_k8s_auth

Status.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

outputSchema.description: Response for checking authorization for a Kubernetes resource.

result.description: The result of auth can-i check.

tools.description: Retrieves events from a Kubernetes cluster. This is similar to running `kubectl events`.

inputSchema.description: Request message for KubeEvents.

allNamespaces.description: Optional. If true, retrieve events from all namespaces.

limit.description: Optional. The maximum number of events to return. If not specified, 500 is used.

name.description: Optional. The name of the resource to retrieve events for.

namespace.description: Optional. The namespace of the resource. If not specified and all_namespaces is false, "default" is used.

resourceType.description: Optional. The type of the resource to retrieve events for.

tools.name: list_k8s_events

tools.description: Gets one or more Kubernetes resources from a cluster. Resources can be filtered by type, name, namespace, and label selectors. Returns the resources in YAML format. This is similar to running `kubectl get`.

inputSchema.description: Request for retrieving Kubernetes resources.

customColumns.description: Optional. The field mask to specify columns to display. Use a single "*" to get all fields. When both custom_columns and output_format are specified, output_format is ignored.

fieldSelector.description: Optional. A field selector to filter resources.

labelSelector.description: Optional. A label selector to filter resources.

name.description: Optional. The name of the resource to retrieve. If not specified, all resources of the given type are returned.

namespace.description: Optional. The namespace of the resource. If not specified, all namespaces are searched.

outputFormat.description: Optional. The output format. One of: (table, wide, yaml, json). If not specified, defaults to table. When both custom_columns and output_format are specified, output_format is ignored.
x-google-enum-descriptions: If not specified, defaults to table.

parent.description: Required. The cluster, which owns this collection of resources. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/clusters/{cluster}

resourceType.description: Required. The type of resource to retrieve. Kubernetes resource/kind name in singular form, lower case. e.g. "pod", "deployment", "service".

tools.name: get_k8s_resource

Status.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

outputSchema.description: Response for retrieving Kubernetes resources.

output.description: The output of the command in the requested format. It may contain resources in YAML or JSON format, or a table in plain text, or errors.

tools.description: Gets cluster endpoint information. This is similar to running `kubectl cluster-info`.

inputSchema.description: Request message for KubeClusterInfo.

clusterInfo.description: The cluster info of the resource. Displays address and port information about the Kubernetes control plane and other services running within the cluster such as CoreDNS and Metrics-server. Example: "The Kubernetes control plane is at 10.128.0.10:6443. CoreDNS is running at https://192.0.2.1:6443/api/v1/namespaces/kube-system/services/kube-dns:dns/proxy Metrics-server is running at 127.0.0.1:8080"

errors.description: Errors encountered during cluster info retrieval.

tools.description: Lists GKE clusters in a given project and location. Location can be a region, zone, or '-' for all locations.

inputSchema.description: MCPListClustersRequest lists clusters.

parent.description: Required. The parent (project and location) where the clusters will be listed. Specified in the format `projects/*/locations/*`. Location "-" matches all zones and all regions.

readMask.description: Optional. The field mask to specify the fields to be returned in the response. Use a single "*" to get all fields. Default: clusters.autopilot,clusters.createTime,clusters.currentMasterVersion,clusters.currentNodeCount,clusters.currentNodeVersion,clusters.description,clusters.endpoint,clusters.fleet,clusters.location,clusters.name,clusters.network,clusters.nodePools.name,clusters.releaseChannel,clusters.resourceLabels,clusters.selfLink,clusters.status,clusters.statusMessage,clusters.subnetwork,missingZones.

tools.name: list_clusters

Status.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

outputSchema.description: Response for listing clusters in JSON format. The full cluster object can be found at https://cloud.google.com/container-engine/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.clusters.

clusters.description: A string representing the ListClustersResponse object.

tools.description: Creates a new GKE cluster in a given project and location. It's recommended to read the [GKE documentation](https://docs.cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/configuration-overview) to understand cluster configuration options. Cluster creation will default to Autopilot mode, as recommended by GKE best practices. If the user explicitly wants to create a Standard cluster, you need to set autopilot.enabled=false in the cluster configuration. This is similar to running `gcloud container clusters create-auto` or `gcloud container clusters create`.

inputSchema.description: MCPCreateClusterRequest creates a new cluster.

cluster.description: Required. A [cluster resource](https://cloud.google.com/container-engine/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.clusters) represented as a string using JSON format.

parent.description: Required. The parent (project and location) where the cluster will be created. Specified in the format `projects/*/locations/*`.

tools.name: create_cluster

Status.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

outputSchema.description: MCPOperation wraps the GKE Operation with an errors field.

operation.description: JSON string of the GKE Operation object. See: https://docs.cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.operations

tools.description: Gets logs from a Kubernetes container in a pod. This is similar to running `kubectl logs`.

inputSchema.description: KubeLogsRequest is the request message for retrieving Kubernetes logs.

allContainers.description: Optional. If true, retrieve logs from all containers in the pod.

container.description: Optional. The name of the container to retrieve logs from. If not specified, logs from the first container are returned.

name.description: Required. The name of the resource to retrieve logs from. This can be a pod name (e.g. "my-pod") or a type/name (e.g. "deployment/my-deployment"). If a type is not specified, "pod" is assumed.

namespace.description: Optional. The namespace of the resource. If not specified, "default" is used.

parent.description: Required. The cluster to retrieve logs from. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/clusters/{cluster}

previous.description: Optional. If true, retrieve logs from the previous instantiation of the container.

since.description: Optional. Retrieve logs since this duration ago (e.g. "1h", "10m").

sinceTime.description: Optional. Retrieve logs since this time (RFC3339). e.g. "2024-08-30T06:00:00Z".

tail.description: Optional. The number of lines from the end of the logs to show.

timestamps.description: Optional. If true, include timestamps in the log output.

tools.name: get_k8s_logs

Status.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

outputSchema.description: KubeLogsResponse is the response message for retrieving Kubernetes logs.

tools.description: Applies a Kubernetes manifest to a cluster using server-side apply. This is similar to running `kubectl apply --server-side`.

inputSchema.description: KubeApplyRequest is the request message for applying Kubernetes manifests.

dryRun.description: Optional. If true, run in dry-run mode.

forceConflicts.description: Optional. If true, force conflicts resolution when applying.

parent.description: Required. The cluster to apply the manifest to. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/clusters/{cluster}

yamlManifest.description: Required. The YAML manifest to apply.

tools.name: apply_k8s_manifest

Status.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

outputSchema.description: KubeApplyResponse is the response message for applying Kubernetes manifests.

errors.description: Errors encountered during apply. If this field is populated, some resources may not have been applied.

result.description: Result of the apply operation, e.g., resources created/configured. This might be a summary string or structured data.

tools.description: Deletes a Kubernetes resource from a cluster. This is similar to running `kubectl delete`.

inputSchema.description: Request for deleting Kubernetes resources.

cascade.description: Optional. The cascading deletion policy to use. If not specified, 'background' is used. Valid values are 'background', 'foreground', and 'orphan'.

dryRun.description: Optional. If true, run in dry-run mode.

name.description: Required. The name of the resource to delete.

namespace.description: Optional. The namespace of the resource. If not specified, "default" is used.

parent.description: Required. The cluster, which owns this collection of resources. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/clusters/{cluster}

resourceType.description: Required. The type of resource to delete. Kubernetes resource/kind name in singular form, lower case. e.g. "pod", "deployment", "service".

tools.name: delete_k8s_resource

Status.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

outputSchema.description: Response for deleting Kubernetes resources.

tools.description: Patches a Kubernetes resource. This is similar to running `kubectl patch`.

inputSchema.description: Request for patching a Kubernetes resource.

name.description: Required. The name of the resource to patch.

namespace.description: Optional. The namespace of the resource. If not specified, "default" is used.

parent.description: Required. The cluster to patch the resource in. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/clusters/{cluster}

patch.description: Required. The patch to apply in JSON format.

resourceType.description: Required. The type of resource to patch. e.g. "pods", "deployments", "services".

tools.name: patch_k8s_resource

Status.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.
